questions.question: Derive the formula for mean of grouped data using step deviation method.
questions.answer: Mean = A + Σf·u × h / Σf, where A = assumed mean, u = (x - A)/h, h = class width, f = frequency.

questions.question: Derive the formula for median of grouped data using cumulative frequency.
questions.answer: Median = L + [(N/2 - CF)/f] × h, where L = lower boundary of median class, N = total frequency, CF = cumulative frequency before median class, f = frequency of median class, h = class width.

questions.question: Derive the formula for mode of grouped data using frequency table.
questions.answer: Mode = L + [(f1 - f0)/(2f1 - f0 - f2)] × h, where L = lower boundary of modal class, f1 = frequency of modal class, f0 = frequency of previous class, f2 = frequency of next class, h = class width.

questions.question: Explain the relationship between mean, median and mode in a moderately skewed distribution.
questions.answer: Empirical relation: Mode ≈ 3 Median - 2 Mean; If Mean > Median > Mode, distribution is positively skewed; If Mean < Median < Mode, distribution is negatively skewed.

questions.question: How do you estimate median from a less than cumulative frequency ogive?
questions.answer: Locate N/2 on Y-axis, draw a horizontal line to meet the ogive, drop vertical to X-axis; intersection gives median.

questions.question: How do you estimate median from a more than cumulative frequency ogive?
questions.answer: Locate N/2 on Y-axis (from top), draw horizontal line to meet ogive, drop vertical to X-axis; intersection gives median.

questions.question: Explain how to find mode from a histogram using graphical method.
questions.answer: Draw histogram, identify modal class (highest bar), draw diagonals from top of adjacent bars to top of modal bar; intersection with modal bar gives mode.

questions.question: Explain the step deviation method for calculating mean with an example.
questions.answer: Choose an assumed mean A, calculate u = (x - A)/h for all class midpoints, multiply by frequency f, sum Σf·u, divide by Σf and add A to get mean.

questions.question: Explain less than and more than ogives and their uses.
questions.answer: Less than ogive: cumulative frequencies plotted against upper class boundaries; More than ogive: cumulative frequencies plotted from top downward. Used to estimate median, percentiles, quartiles.

questions.question: Define percentile and explain how to find the p-th percentile from grouped data.
questions.answer: Percentile: value below which p% of observations fall. Pth percentile = L + [(pN/100 - CF)/f] × h, L = lower boundary, N = total frequency, CF = cumulative frequency before class, f = frequency, h = class width.

questions.question: Define quartiles and how to find them from grouped data.
questions.answer: Quartiles divide data into 4 equal parts. Q1 = L + [(N/4 - CF)/f] × h, Q2 = median, Q3 = L + [3N/4 - CF)/f] × h.

questions.question: What is the cumulative frequency of the last class in a distribution?
questions.answer: It equals the total frequency (N) of the data set.

questions.question: How do you calculate mean deviation about the mean for grouped data?
questions.answer: MD = Σf·|x - mean| / Σf, where x = class midpoint, f = frequency.

questions.question: Explain variance and standard deviation for grouped data.
questions.answer: Variance σ² = Σf(x - mean)² / Σf; Standard deviation σ = √σ², x = class midpoint, f = frequency.

questions.question: How do you identify the modal class from a histogram?
questions.answer: The class corresponding to the highest bar in the histogram is the modal class.

questions.question: Explain the difference between grouped and ungrouped data in calculating mean.
questions.answer: Ungrouped mean = sum of observations / number of observations; Grouped mean = sum(f·x)/Σf, where x = class midpoints.

questions.question: How can you estimate the mode from a cumulative frequency graph?
questions.answer: Draw less than and more than ogives, intersection gives median; use formula for mode with modal class.

questions.question: Explain the difference between median and mean using skewness.
questions.answer: In positive skew: mean > median > mode; In negative skew: mean < median < mode; median less affected by extreme values than mean.

questions.question: How can cumulative frequency be used to estimate quartiles?
questions.answer: Q1: cumulative frequency = N/4, Q2: N/2 (median), Q3: 3N/4; use formula: Q = L + [(CF_target - CF)/f] × h.

questions.question: Explain how to calculate coefficient of variation for grouped data.
questions.answer: CV = (standard deviation / mean) × 100%.

questions.question: Explain how to draw frequency polygon from grouped data.
questions.answer: Plot midpoints of each class on X-axis, frequencies on Y-axis, join points with straight lines; extend lines to meet X-axis at first and last midpoint.

questions.question: How do you convert raw data to a frequency table?
questions.answer: Group data into classes, count number of observations in each class, record as frequencies.

questions.question: Explain how to find percentiles from a cumulative frequency graph.
questions.answer: Locate pth percentile on Y-axis, draw horizontal line to meet ogive, drop vertical to X-axis; intersection is pth percentile.

questions.question: What is the importance of using class midpoints in grouped data?
questions.answer: Class midpoints are representative values of classes for estimating mean, variance, and other statistics.

questions.question: Explain how to calculate mean deviation about median for grouped data.
questions.answer: MD = Σf·|x - median| / Σf, where x = class midpoint, f = frequency.
